notes: GICS sector = reporting classification. Economic profile = analytical normalization bucket. Review annually.

AAPL.rationale: GICS industry Consumer Electronics understates AAPL economics. Services segment (~25% revenue, ~70% gross margin) drives blended margin expansion and ecosystem lock-in. Not comparable to pure consumer hardware.

SPOT.rationale: GICS industry Internet Content & Information would map to platform_technology, but Spotify's economics are content/streaming — gross margins ~30%, content costs dominate. Closer to digital_media than platform.

DIS.rationale: Disney is a conglomerate (parks + streaming + licensing). Entertainment GICS industry maps to digital_media which is the closest fit for V1. Review in V2 for conglomerate-specific profile.

AXP.rationale: AmEx is closer to banking than payments_network — it carries credit risk on its own balance sheet unlike Visa/Mastercard which are pure networks.

GE.rationale: Post-spinoff GE Aerospace is a pure aviation engine and defense company. Aerospace & Defense GICS industry string correctly maps here.

banking.note: NIM and ROE are primary metrics. Standard margin analysis structurally invalid. net_cash disabled: customer deposits are operating liabilities, not leverage, so cash-minus-debt is meaningless for banks.

insurance.note: Combined ratio is primary metric. Standard margin analysis structurally invalid. net_cash disabled: float and reserves are operating liabilities, not leverage.

reit.note: FFO is primary metric. FCF margin analysis misleading for REITs.

energy_upstream.note: EDGAR gross profit for E&P excludes DD&A and depletion (the largest cost of an upstream producer), so reported gross margin is structurally inflated and not comparable across industries.

platform_technology.description: Network effects, software-like scaling, high gross margins. GICS often misclassifies as Communication Services.

semiconductor_platform.description: Chip design with platform lock-in. High gross margins, cyclical but moaty.

enterprise_software.description: B2B software with sticky revenue, high gross margins, moderate growth.

consumer_technology_ecosystem.description: Hardware entry point, services margin engine, ecosystem lock-in moat. AAPL model specifically.

consumer_electronics.description: Pure or predominantly hardware consumer electronics. Lower margins, more cyclical.

digital_media.description: Content and streaming. Subscriber growth alongside margins.

traditional_telecom.description: Low growth, high capex, utility-like economics.

pharma_innovator.description: High gross margins, R&D-heavy, patent cliff risk.

medtech_device.description: Medical devices. Mid gross margins, recurring procedure revenue.

consumer_staples_brand.description: Branded consumer staples with pricing power and stable margins.

retail_discretionary.description: Consumer discretionary retail. Cyclical, moderate margins.

industrial_manufacturer.description: Industrial manufacturing. Mid margins, capex heavy, GDP-linked growth.

aerospace_defense.description: Aerospace and defense. Government contracts, predictable revenue.

energy_upstream.description: Pure upstream oil and gas E&P. Commodity-driven.

integrated_energy.description: Integrated oil and gas. Upstream + downstream + retail.

energy_midstream.description: Pipeline and midstream. Toll road economics.

reit.description: Real estate investment trust. FFO-based economics.

payments_network.description: Card network and payments infrastructure. Extremely high margins, asset-light.

asset_management.description: Fee-based asset management. High margins, AUM-driven.

insurance.description: Insurance. Combined ratio is primary metric.

unknown.description: Profile could not be determined. Neutral multipliers applied. Review required.
